item.title: The Mid-Year Career Check-In You Shouldn't Skip
item.body:
## Halfway through the year — and a perfect time to pause

The end of June marks the true midpoint of the year, which makes it an ideal, and often overlooked, moment to take stock of your career. Most people do this in January, ride the momentum for a few weeks, and forget about it. A mid-year check-in catches you before the goals you set in the winter quietly slip away — and while there's still half a year to act on them.

## Four questions worth asking yourself

1. **Am I moving toward the goals I set, or just staying busy?** Activity and progress aren't the same thing. Look honestly at whether your daily work is building toward something or simply filling the calendar.
2. **What have I learned this year that I couldn't do before?** If the answer is "not much," that's a signal — either to seek new challenges where you are or to look elsewhere.
3. **Is my compensation keeping pace?** Mid-year is a smart time to quietly research market rates for your role, so you walk into any future conversation informed rather than guessing.
4. **Do people know what I've accomplished?** Good work that no one sees rarely gets rewarded. Note your wins now, while they're fresh, so you're ready when reviews come.

## The summer hiring myth

Many job seekers go quiet in summer, assuming nobody hires until fall. That belief is more convenient than true. Hiring continues year-round, and the summer lull in *applicants* can actually mean less competition for the roles that stay open. If you've been considering a move, a slower season is often a better time to stand out, not a worse one.

## Small steps that compound

You don't need a dramatic reinvention. A productive mid-year check-in can be as simple as:

- **Updating your résumé and profile** while your recent accomplishments are still sharp in memory.
- **Reconnecting with one former colleague** over coffee or a message — the least stressful and most reliable form of networking.
- **Picking one concrete skill** to build over the summer, and blocking out the time to actually do it.

## The takeaway

Careers rarely change in a single dramatic leap. They shift through small, deliberate adjustments made at the right moments. The middle of the year is exactly such a moment — quiet enough to think clearly, early enough to still change your trajectory. Take an hour this week to look up from the daily work and ask where it's actually taking you.
